title: Remove the Home Screen widget feature
description:
Owner wants to remove the Basket Home Screen widget — verbatim: "let's remove all of the basket home page widget style functions. I think we just don't need it." Rationale given: it's just not needed. (Note the phrase 'widget style functions' is a little ambiguous via voice — it most likely means the whole Home Screen widget feature/its code, but it COULD narrowly mean only the widget's styling/rendering. Do NOT assume; let conversion confirm scope with the owner: entire widget extension vs. just styling.)

IMPORTANT CONTEXT — this reverses very recently completed work. The widget was built out across a whole task chain that is now all done: T026 (App Group + shared SwiftData container, needs-human, done — the foundation), T033 (view-only widget small+medium, done; T029 was a failed duplicate), T071 (interactive tap-to-check-off widget, done), T072 (quick-add '+' + add/combined widget variants, done). It also went through notable Swift 6 / test friction recently (WidgetReload seam, CheckOffItemIntent predicate). Removing it discards that.

Code surface to remove/untangle (for conversion to scope):
- BasketWidget/ (the whole extension target, 11 files): BasketWidget.swift, BasketWidgetBundle.swift, BasketWidgetEntryView.swift, BasketWidgetProvider.swift, BasketAddWidget.swift + BasketAddWidgetEntryView.swift + BasketAddWidgetProvider.swift, BasketCombinedWidget.swift + BasketCombinedWidgetEntryView.swift, BasketWidget.entitlements, Info.plist.
- Sources/Services/WidgetReload.swift (the reload seam), Sources/Services/BasketWidgetSnapshot.swift (snapshot builder), Sources/Support/BasketWidgetKind.swift (widget kind id).
- Sources/Services/CheckOffItemIntent.swift — this AppIntent exists ONLY for the interactive widget (tap-to-check-off); it goes away with the widget.
- project.yml — the BasketWidget extension target + its embedding into the app target (dependencies), plus any widget-only build settings/entitlements.
- Tests: any widget/CheckOffItemIntent tests (e.g. under Tests/ and UITests/) that exercise the widget.

DO NOT remove (separate features that merely touch the widget):
- Sources/Services/AddToBasketIntent.swift is the Siri 'Add to Basket' App Shortcut (a DIFFERENT feature) — it calls WidgetReload.reloadTimelines() after a Siri add. Removing the widget means those WidgetReload calls become removable no-ops, but the Siri add intent itself stays.
- The App Group + shared SwiftData container (T026/AppGroup/AppSchema.makeSharedContainer) also backs Siri; check whether anything else still needs it before ripping it out — likely keep it for Siri.

Also worth deciding at conversion: whether to keep the 'basket://add' deep link (T028) that the quick-add widget button used — it may still be useful from other entry points, or may become dead once the widget is gone. README + CLAUDE.md project map reference the widget and would need updating in the same change.
